YOUTUBE SWIPE FILE
We’ve provided a wide range of content to represent the over 5000+ videos Grant
Cardone has posted on YouTube.
To follow Grant’s channel through the years you can go to 10xresource.com/ytjourney
and sort by oldest.
Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf1
Lesson Learned: This was the very first video Grant ever created specifically for
YouTube! You’ll notice that it wasn’t at all high production quality, but that Grant did
take it very seriously. It is also worth noting that this number of views only came years
later. The video initially only got a few hundred views.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 12 --

Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf2
Lesson Learned: This was Grant’s first big breakthrough video in regards to views. As
you’ll see, it was actually an old clip he used from one of his seminars, and the quality is
extremely low. This video was successful, because it ranked high for the terms
“automotive sales” and “auto sales”.
Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf3
Lesson Learned: In this video Grant introduces his wife Elena to his social media world.
It wasn’t pre-meditated, and in fact, Elena didn’t even realize they were recording.
What matters is that you can hear her conviction that the world needed to know Grant
and that they soon would. This is the beginning of the Cardone Empire.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 12 --

Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf4
Lesson Learned: This video is significant because it was the first time the 10X Rule ever
appeared on video. This was actually a year before 10X Rule was even published. While
the video was Grant repurposing a training call and was not especially popular, it
shows off his relentless promotional style.
Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf5
Lesson Learned: Created by a fan of Grant’s, this video was the first posted to his
channel that used multi-media and b-roll footage for the purpose of inspiring. All
previous production videos were sales related. The result was a major spike in viewers
from previous videos.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 12 --

Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf6
Lesson Learned: This was Grant’s first video that did over a quarter million views.
While the topic was someone in line with previous videos, you’ll see a few new
strategies emerging. The title is presented in an “in your face” fashion, as opposed to
something traditional like “How to not be lazy”. Also he plays with a unique intro.
Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf7
Lesson Learned: In this clip from HGTV’s show Selling LA, Grant has a major
breakthrough in starting to leverage major media. He took the opportunity to sell his
property on television, which gave him a tremendous amount of free publicity. On the
show he did a tremendous job positioning himself like an authority.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 12 --

Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf8
Lesson Learned: While Grant’s channel was trending up at this point, many of his
videos were under 1000 views. This was an example of Grant quickly acting on a
current event. People who were searching about this TV show contestant the next
morning, Grant was ready and positioned to capitalize for a huge spike in views.
Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf9
Lesson Learned: Grant proved a powerful point by having a 6 year old reciting his sales
training. This video was provided by a fan. By mixing up the format and coming from
an intriguing angle, this video got a big spike in views and engagement.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 12 --

Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf10
Lesson Learned: While this video wasn’t a breakthrough in views, it is a great lesson of
how to leverage media. As you can see in the video, by sharing this story on his Twitter,
Grant was attracted national media attention. He has been able to leverage multiple
real-time situations like this in his career. Now he flies private!
Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf11
Lesson Learned: In this video Grant tackles a real world problem/opportunity. What
made it popular was the fact that Grant took the audience with him. If he would have
shared the same topic from a studio, it would have never had the same effect.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 12 --

Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf12
Lesson Learned: While this video is nothing out of the ordinary, it was a turning point.
In his 51st “Grant Rant” he touched on a topic that would become a mainstay: how to
become a millionaire. More importantly, he immediately followed it up in his next video
titled “Work like a millionaire” which got 97,000 views!
Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf13
Lesson Learned: Grant breaks the 1,000,000 views mark with this video and went on to
do nearly 2 million! The big lesson is that this video took zero work. It was a paid
speaking gig, and Grant always negotiates to have rights to use the footage.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 12 --

Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf14
Lesson Learned: After building his YouTube channel for years, and strategically
capitalizing on every opportunity for exposure possible, Grant lands his own TV show.
While National Geographic channel ended up cancelling Turnaround King, it was a
major milestone in Grant’s career.
Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf15
Lesson Learned: This was Grant’s first livestream on Youtube. As you’ll notice, what he
did was simply turn on a camera while he recorded his radio show, the Cardone Zone.
By giving the YouTube algorithm what it wanted (live streams) Grant saw a boost in his
overall viewership for regular videos too.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 12 --

Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf16
Lesson Learned: Another classic example of persistence paying off, Grant hit a big one.
This worked because it is on the edge of his niche, and a topic that is highly searched.
Again, Grant was smart enough to leverage the success of this video. The next video he
put out was called “How to Get Your Dream Job”. It got 700,000 views!
Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf17
Lesson Learned: This video spiked way up in views to his 3 million! By sharing a live
sales call, rather than simply sharing training tips, Grant was able to grab a lot more
attention than he had with previous sales training videos. Also notice the well placed
link in the description which generated lots of Cardone U sales.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 12 --

Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf18
Lesson Learned: Unlike most trainers and experts, Grant is not afraid to share what he
teaches at his seminars. This was Grant speaking about how to get attention in the
marketplace. It got million of views and is definitely worth a watch!
Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf19
Lesson Learned: Grant’s #1 all-time organic video is an all-time classic. He used his
morning sales team training as an opportunity to create content. The video starts with
Grant walking, inviting the audience to join. The lesson is simple, and powerful. It also
performed well because Grant’s audience loves the theme: becoming a millionaire!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 12 --

Video Link: 10xresource.com/ytsf20
Lesson Learned: This video represents another major breakthrough in Grant’s
YouTube channel: the use of Shorts. Because Grant was smart enough to capitalize on
the new feature, he saw a massive spike not only in views, but subscribers and
engagement. As you can see, this video got over 348,000 likes!
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
